title: Trade and International Business: Research & Analysis | CSIS
text:
Trade and International Business

Reciprocal tariffs, threats to global supply chains, and comparative manufacturing advantages have disrupted the established global trade system. CSIS scholars provide analysis and commentary on the international trading system and its institutions.

Image

Photo: Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images

Anthropic and the Export Control Dilemma

In this week's column, Bill Reinsch discusses the use of export controls on Anthropic's latest models and examines the tradeoff between measures that may hinder industry competitiveness and the risk that, without such controls, advanced technologies could fall into the hands of adversaries.

Commentary
by
William Alan Reinsch

— June 24, 2026

Three Nations, One Table: North American Agriculture under USMCA

Join the Americas Program for a conference on USMCA and the future of North American food and agriculture. Experts will discuss food affordability, the successes and challenges of agricultural integration, and the risks that broader trade tensions pose to the agreement’s agricultural framework ahead of the 2026 review.

Event

— June 24, 2026

Anthropic and the Export Control Dilemma

In this week's column, Bill Reinsch discusses the use of export controls on Anthropic's latest models and examines the tradeoff between measures that may hinder industry competitiveness and the risk that, without such controls, advanced technologies could fall into the hands of adversaries.

Commentary
by
William Alan Reinsch

— June 24, 2026

Preventing Iran’s Military Reconstitution

Iran’s ability to reconstitute its military depends on its ability to import key materials and equipment. With the UAE’s role as Iran’s central reexport hub up in the air, policymakers have a narrow window to identify and disrupt new procurement routes as they take shape. 

Report
by
CSIS Scholars

— June 23, 2026

The Trade Guys

USMCA Review, G7 Summit, and the EU-U.S. Trade Deal

Bill and Scott talk USMCA review, the G7 Summit, and the European Parliament's EU-U.S. trade deal.

Image

Southeast Asia Radio

Vietnam’s Evolving Foreign Policy with Le Hong Hiep

This week, Greg and Elina were joined by Le Hong Hiep to discuss recent developments in Vietnam’s foreign policy after To Lam’s visits to the Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand. Patal and Japhet cover the latest developments, from corruption investigations in Indonesia and the Philippines to Chinese engagement in the region.

Podcast Episode
by

Gregory B. Poling,

Elina Noor,

Le Hong Hiep,
and
Japhet Quitzon

— June 18, 2026

Image

The Customs Executive Order: Dealing with Downstream Consequences

In this week's column, Bill Reinsch discusses the implications of the Trump administration's "Strengthening Customs Enforcement" executive order, highlighting the increased costs and compliance challenges it may create for importers.

Commentary
by
William Alan Reinsch

— June 17, 2026

The Department of Commerce Restricted Access to Anthropic’s Latest Models. What Comes Next?

On June 12, the Department of Commerce imposed export controls on Anthropic’s latest models, causing abrupt disruptions in service and raising questions about future U.S. government AI policies. Our experts discuss next steps and alternate pathways.

Critical Questions
by

Kate Koren,

Kevin Kurland,
and
Aalok Mehta

— June 16, 2026

Energy Considerations for EXIM Reauthorization

Rejuvenating EXIM will be crucial to U.S. competitiveness as export credit agencies play an increasingly important role in global energy finance.

Critical Questions
by
Ray Cai

— June 15, 2026

Half Right on Forced Labor Tariffs

In this week’s column, Bill Reinsch warns that weak evidence behind the USTR’s trade investigations could expose the administration’s tariff decisions to legal challenges.

Commentary
by
William Alan Reinsch

— June 10, 2026

Unpacking Brazil’s Latest Effort to Regulate Digital Markets

Evan Brown and Hannah Amran unpack Brazil’s latest bold attempt to restructure the country’s digital competition landscape, Bill 4675/2025, the Fair Competition Act for Digital Markets.

Blog Post
by

Evan Brown
and
Hannah Amran

— June 5, 2026
